revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Arashima Kofun Cluster]][[ja:荒島古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Sanin Expressway -01.jpg|location={{ill|Yasugi|qid=Q851114|1=Yasugi, Shimane}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42972222222222|N|133.19861111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}}}
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofuns in Yasugi, Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Arashima Kofun cluster | native_name =荒島古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Sanin Expressway -01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = San'in Expressway Yonago Nishi IC - Higashi Izumo IC (Yasugi Road). Above the tunnel, Kofun No. 1 of the Shiotsuyama Kofun Cluster, which was developed as the ``Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill''. | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Arashima Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|47|N|133|11|55|E|display=inline, title|qq=}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yasugi, Shimane|en|Yasugi, Shimane|qid=Q851114|lt=Yasugi, Shimane|1=Yasugi, Shimane}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} to [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No facilities | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|47|N|133|11|55|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Arashima Kofun cluster'''|荒島古墳群|||qq=}} is a group of {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi|1=Yayoi period}} to [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Aratori-cho and Kujira-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Yasugi, Shimane|en|Yasugi, Shimane|qid=Q851114|lt= Yasugi|1=Yasugi, Shimane}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936 with the area under protection expanded in 1999. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title= 荒島古墳群|trans-title= Amidaji Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Arashima Kofun cluster is located on the Arashima Hills facing {{ill|Nakaumi|en|Nakaumi|qid=Q1077275|lt=Nakaumi|1=Nakaumi}} and consists of the Taisei Kofun, four of the Tsukuriyama Tumuli, and eight of the Shiotsuyama Tumuli. The Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 (造山古墳1号墳) is a large {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=}}-style square tumulus from the early Kofun period and was designed a National Historic Site in 1936. However, as the Arashima Hills was later found to contain a large concentration of Kofuns dating from the late Yayoi period to the Kofun period, the area was expanded in 1999 and renamed to the "Arashima Kofun Cluster" as the Kofuns were considered to be important in understanding the evolution of tumuli in the {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|qid=Q582846|lt=Izumo region|1=Izumo Province}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The Taisei Kofun (大成古墳) was discovered during land clearing in 1911, and is also as a large rectangular tomb from the early Kofun period. The tumulus is approximately 60 meters on each side and approximately 6 meters high. From the pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in the center of the mound, a triangular-rimmed divine beast {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, a large iron sword with a ring-shaped head, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery, and other items were [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/> The Tsukuriyama tumuli to the west side consists of four Kofuns, including the aforementioned Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 and Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 3, which a similar large rectangular tomb. Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 is approximately 60 meters on a side and approximately 7 meters in height, and contained two pit-style stone burial chambers. It was excavated in 1936 and 1938, during which time a triangular-rimmed bronze mirror of divine beasts, a square-shaped bronze mirror of four gods, a spindle-shaped stone product, a glass tube bead, and iron swords were discovered.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 3 measures 38 by 36 meters. It was excavated in 1960 and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included a bronze mirror with beveled edges of two gods and two beasts, a jasper bead, a small glass bead, an iron sword, and a spear. At the highest point of the hill is Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 3, a 50-meter long "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}}) mound, and the much smaller Tsukuriyama Kofun No. , both of which were trial excavated in 1990. Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 5 is located slightly further away, and is outside the borders of the National Historic SIte. The Shiotsuyama tumuli on the east side has eleven confirmed tumuli, with Shiotsuyama No.1 being a large {{nihongo|''Yosumi-tosshutsu-gata''|四隅突出形|qq=}}-style tumuli with four protruding corners from the late Yayoi period. This is a variation of the ''hōfun''-style square tumulus with polygonal-shaped protrusions on each of its four corners. This style is unique to the {{ill|Kibi Province|en|Kibi Province|qid=Q1155128|lt=Kibi|1=Kingdom of Kibi}}, San'in and {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|qid=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}}s of Japan. During the construction of the {{ill|San'in Expressway|en|San'in Expressway|qid=Q5608657|lt=San'in Expressway|1=San'in Expressway}}, a tunnel was constructed underneath the tumulus in order to preserve it. Shiotsuyama No. 6, and Shiotsuyama No. 10, which are rectangular tombs from the early Kofun period, still retain the remnants of the four-cornered shape, and are among the largest of this type. <ref name="Shiseki"/> This group of Kofuns was not discovered until the late 1970s. The site is approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Arashima Station|en|Arashima Station|qid=Q4784373|lt=Arashima Station|1=Arashima Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is maintained by Yasugi City as a historical park: Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill. == Detailed Descriptions of Key Tombs == === Chūsenji Kofun Group === {{main|Chūsenji Kofun Group|qq=}} The Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups of this site were designated as a national historic site in {{ill|1971|en|1971|qid=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}}.<ref>{{sfn|近藤|1972|qq=}}</ref> The Chūsenji subgroup contains 16 identified mounds,<ref>{{sfn|出雲考古学研究会|1985|p=9|qq=}}</ref> primarily {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/four-cornered protruding mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. === Anyōji Tomb Group === Three mounds were identified,<ref>{{sfn|勝部|1980|qq=}}</ref> two of which were classified as four-cornered protruding mounds. Unfortunately, none remain intact.<ref>{{sfn|出雲考古学研究会|1985|p=14|qq=}}</ref> === Shiotsuyama Tomb Group === Designated as part of the Arashima Kofun Group in {{ill|1999|en|1999|qid=Q2091|lt=1999|1=1999}}, this site comprises 11 mounds, including four-cornered protruding mounds and a distinctive square mound.<ref>{{sfn|島根県教委|1996|p=8|qq=}}</ref> === Ōnari Kofun === This is an early Kofun period square mound with burial goods such as {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.<ref>{{sfn|出雲考古学研究会|1985|p=29|qq=}}</ref> === Tsukuriyama Kofun Group === {{main|Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|qq=}} Comprising five mounds, including two early Kofun period square mounds, this site was preserved and is now part of the "Koda Izumo Ōryō no Oka" historical park.<ref name="pref" /> ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]] *{{ill|Chūsenji Kofun Cluster|en|Chūsenji Kofun Cluster|qid=Q103982915|lt=Chūsenji Kofun Group|1=Nakazenji Kofun Group}} *{{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|qq=}} *{{ill|Nishidani Tumulus cluster|en|Nishidani Tumulus cluster|qid=Q11629514|lt=Nishidani Tomb Group|1=Nishidani Kofun cluster}} *{{ill|Four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-cornered protruding mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[http://yasugi-arashima.com/shutsudo.html Arashima District Revitalization Promotion Councii] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21087 Shimane Prefecture Tourism Federation] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} Arashima Kofuns
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
